name: PowerGrid Sustainability Budget
description: Approximate safe resource budgets for checking whether PowerGrid crowds out normal Stardew play. Weekly income is an expected renewable or routine acquisition amount, not a total stockpile. MaxPowerGridShare is the share PowerGrid may consume before it starts feeling intrusive.

stages.name: First Powered Corner
stages.description: Early starter grid. PowerGrid should be cheap enough to try without eating the whole mining or crafting budget.

Wood.notes: Wood is still needed for buildings, chests, tappers, and farm expansion.

Hardwood.notes: Hardwood is protected early because stable and tool goals compete for it.

Fiber.notes: Fiber is renewable but still used by tea saplings, wild bait, grass starter, and utility crafting.

Moss.notes: Moss is available before Green Rain but should be treated as a light optional bonus early.

Sap.notes: Sap should remain mostly free for fertilizer and normal crafting.

Coal.notes: Coal competes with bars, preserve jars, bee houses, and normal machines.

Copper Bar.notes: Copper still feeds tools, tappers, sprinklers, and basic farm infrastructure.

Iron Bar.notes: Iron is a major early bottleneck and should not be swallowed by power.

Refined Quartz.notes: Refined Quartz should stay a light tech flavor cost, not a wall.

Battery Pack.notes: Early battery packs are rare and should be used sparingly.

stages.name: Winter Y1 Artisan Room
stages.description: First serious powered room. Costs can matter, but fuel should not take over the weekly routine.

Wood.notes: Winter wood is available, but buildings and kegs still compete for it.

Hardwood.notes: Hardwood should remain mostly protected before late-game access improves.

Fiber.notes: Fiber can support some fuel, but should not become mandatory cleanup grind.

Moss.notes: Moss is easier after Green Rain, but not every player farms it deliberately.

Sap.notes: Sap can support some fuel, but should not erase fertilizer usage.

Coal.notes: Coal pressure matters because bars and vanilla machines still need it.

Copper Bar.notes: Copper is less tight, but still part of normal expansion.

Iron Bar.notes: Iron demand should feel industrial, not punitive.

Gold Bar.notes: Gold starts becoming available, but is not the core early grid material.

Battery Pack.notes: Battery packs remain uncommon before larger lightning rod setups.

stages.name: Summer Y2 Mid Shed
stages.description: Combustion starts scaling production. Biofuel should be useful without becoming the whole farm economy.

Wood.notes: Wood remains important for artisan expansion.

Hardwood.notes: Hardwood is still a premium resource.

Fiber.notes: Fiber is a good Biofuel candidate because it is renewable and often underused.

Moss.notes: Moss can make Biofuel more efficient, but Green Rain should not be mandatory.

Sap.notes: Biofuel may use Sap, but should leave room for fertilizer and crafting.

Coal.notes: Coal should not become the only thing the player mines for.

Copper Bar.notes: Copper pressure is acceptable if it is mostly a one-time setup cost.

Iron Bar.notes: Iron is the industrial backbone, but large jumps should be staged.

Gold Bar.notes: Gold can support mid-game tech, but should not dominate.

Iridium Bar.notes: Iridium is available but still meaningful.

Refined Quartz.notes: Quartz is allowed to matter for tech, but not block whole rooms.

Battery Pack.notes: Battery packs can support batteries and conduits, but should not be disposable fuel yet.

stages.name: Sample Big Shed Conversion
stages.description: Late Year 2 big shed benchmark. Large setup costs can bite, but ongoing fuel should be sustainable.

Wood.notes: Wood should remain available for sheds, kegs, and farm projects.

Hardwood.notes: Hardwood can support advanced machines, but is still not disposable.

Fiber.notes: Fiber can be a major biomass input by late Year 2.

Moss.notes: Moss is useful if the player keeps mossy tree areas, but should stay optional.

Sap.notes: Biofuel can become a real Sap sink, but not the only use for Sap.

Coal.notes: Coal fuel pressure should stay below normal mining comfort.

Copper Bar.notes: Copper should mainly be a setup material by this point.

Iron Bar.notes: Iron pressure should encourage planning, not stall the mod.

Gold Bar.notes: Gold is fair for mid-to-late infrastructure.

Iridium Bar.notes: Iridium costs should signal late-game investment.

Refined Quartz.notes: Quartz should be noticeable but not exhausting.

Battery Pack.notes: Battery packs should remain strategic components.

stages.name: Mature Y3 Organized Conversion
stages.description: Real mature save conversion benchmark. This stage may spend large stockpiles once, but ongoing fuel should still respect the wider save.

Wood.notes: Mature saves can spend more wood, but should not empty normal project reserves.

Hardwood.notes: Hardwood still deserves protection even late.

Fiber.notes: Fiber is allowed to carry a meaningful Biofuel role in mature saves.

Moss.notes: Moss can support efficient Biofuel, but should not demand constant Green Rain farming.

Sap.notes: Sap is the key Biofuel risk; PowerGrid should not require full-time Sap farming.

Coal.notes: Coal may support power, but bars and machines still compete for it.

Copper Bar.notes: Copper should be manageable in mature conversion.

Iron Bar.notes: Iron can be a major conversion cost, but should not require weeks of grind.

Gold Bar.notes: Gold should be comfortable for mature saves.

Iridium Bar.notes: Iridium is allowed to gate advanced conversion.

Refined Quartz.notes: Quartz should not be the main pain point.

Battery Pack.notes: Battery packs should support infrastructure and future power-cell ideas.

stages.name: Late Y3 Density Wing
stages.description: Premium radioactive density stage. This should feel expensive and special, but not absurd once the save is already deep into endgame production.

Wood.notes: Wood should still leave room for sheds, casks, and normal late projects.

Hardwood.notes: Hardwood remains protected even in premium setups.

Fiber.notes: Fiber can comfortably support biomass fuel when the player has a mature farm loop.

Moss.notes: Moss stays optional support, not a required treadmill.

Sap.notes: Sap should remain mostly free for fertilizer and side crafting.

Coal.notes: Coal can support premium infrastructure, but should not become a permanent chore.

Copper Bar.notes: Copper should be painless at this point.

Iron Bar.notes: Iron is still a real industrial input, but should not stall a density upgrade.

Gold Bar.notes: Gold is fair material for premium machinery.

Iridium Bar.notes: Iridium can gate advanced conversion without turning into a wall.

Refined Quartz.notes: Quartz should be noticeable but not the reason the upgrade dies.

Battery Pack.notes: Battery packs remain premium components, not throwaway fuel.

Radioactive Bar.notes: Radioactive bars should support a premium density route, but stay rare enough to feel special.
